extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won Stage 3 sprint by timing final turn extremely late on rising cobbled finish with roundabouts.
tactical_patterns: POGACAR bridged to intermediate sprint for bonus seconds with ALAPHILIPPE/GEOGHEGAN-HART marking, revealing GC riders hunting every second available.
BORA's ACKERMANN plan involved HIRT fatiguing sprinters teams pre-finale, suggesting domestique sacrifice as legitimate sprint leadout tactic on uphill finishes.
EWAN delayed sprint until final roundabout on cobbled rise while others launched early, exploiting technical finish geometry over raw power.
Sprinter teams' chase of breakaway with 50km remaining backfired, leaving leadout trains compromised for technical cobbled finale.

rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: actively hunting 3-second intermediate bonuses mid-stage despite overall lead, suggesting genuine concern about Ganna's 11-second deficit
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN-HART Teo: positive: bridged to GC riders' bonus sprint move and sustained effort, showing Giro form returning earlier than expected
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: negative: spectacular leadout position via Guarnieri but faded to second, confirming disappointing season start continues despite team execution
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: third podium without win, consistent fast finishing without killer instinct on technical finishes this race
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: HIRT's fatigue work yielded outside-top-5 result, suggesting uphill cobbled finishes neutralize pure speed advantage despite tactical investment
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: timed late turn on technical uphill cobbled finish perfectly, demonstrating superior race craft when geometry favors positioning over power
